text:
Every autumn, old Professor Olive would gather his favorite students for an astronomy club observation night. They’d sit out onto the university roof, watching stars drift overhead. One particular night, a heavy fog descended. Outside the observatory, a mysterious old woman was singing a haunting tune. She sang of stars lost and hearts broken.

The students listened. They didnowl’t realize her voice seemed to follow them home. They didn’t realize their own hearts were echoing her words.

When they finally got home, they fell asleep soundly under their windows. The old woman sang silently. When dawn broke, she was gone. But the stars remembered.

That night, owls flew overhead. They landed close to the windows.

One big owl hooted slowly.

Two small owlets responded.

Together they sang an old lullaby.

They flew away.

Professor Olive woke beside his wife. He smiled.

Today’s stars... tomorrow’s whispers.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
